avalancheActivity.highlights: For those venturing off piste a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a storm force northerly wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed in all aspects. In addition further wind slabs will form. These can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, above the tree line. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found in areas close to the tree line and below the tree line. Avalanches can be triggered in near-surface layers and reach large size in isolated cases. Additionally in some places avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
The danger exists primarily in alpine snow sports terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
25 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen.
The wind was violent in some cases. On Friday the wind will be strong to storm force. The wind will transport the new snow significantly.

The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Avalanches can as before be released easily. Restraint is advisable on this first sunny day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs in all aspects. New snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northeasterly wind, sometimes large wind slabs will form by the evening in particular in places that are protected from the wind. In all aspects medium-sized slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of the snowfall. In particular on wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: The old snowpack is largely stable. The new snow will be deposited on a quite favourable old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Thursday: As a consequence of sharply falling temperatures, snowfall and the wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. Where the wind action was less intense, on the other hand, it mainly eroded and sublimated the snow.

In some localities the wind slabs will increase in size moderately on Friday. Along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
In particular gullies and bowls: The fresh snow and very particularly the wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant. Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.

During the day: As a consequence of solar radiation medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are to be expected.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell on Thursday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, in particular in places that are protected from the wind.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The meteorological conditions will facilitate a gradual strengthening of the near-surface layers in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
In the regions exposed to the foehn wind northwest storm:
Wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
As a consequence of low temperatures, and the foehn wind, the snowpack can not consolidate.
In the last two days sometimes large wind slabs formed especially in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

Avalanches can be released, even by small loads in isolated cases or triggered naturally, caution is to be exercised in particular on wind-loaded slopes, and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Especially along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday.
The foehn wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly.
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

As a consequence of low temperatures and the moderate to strong northwesterly wind, the snowpack can not consolidate. The weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack in particular at intermediate and high altitudes.

In particular adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of the precipitation the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase. The fresh wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. They represent the main danger. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. In particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanches can be released naturally and reach very large size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally. The expected fresh snow, which will be heavier in the eastern parts of our region, accompanied by strong winds from the north, will form a snowpack that, especially on sunny slopes, will have a hard surface crust in many places. On shaded slopes, however, in addition to the old weak basal layers, the presence of granular snow and frost crystals has been reported in the surface layers.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs represent the main danger. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on steep slopes dry slab avalanches are possible, even large ones in isolated cases. Avalanches can be released by small loads. This applies in particular above the tree line, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack on very steep shady slopes. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Medium-sized avalanches are possible especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. These can release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. Slopes that have been little used this winter thus far where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form in the course of the day in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in all aspects and generally above the tree line.
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above the tree line.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment: The storm force wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow. As a consequence of low temperatures, and the moderate to strong wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls dry slab avalanches are possible, even quite large ones. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Thursday: As a consequence of sharply falling temperatures, snowfall and the wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. Where the wind action was less intense, on the other hand it mainly eroded and sublimated the snow.
In some localities the wind slabs will increase in size moderately on Friday.
Especially wind-loaded slopes gullies and bowls: The small quantity of fresh snow of yesterday and very particularly the wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.

During the day: As a consequence of solar radiation small and medium-sized dry and moist avalanches are to be expected.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell on Thursday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface. This applies in places that are protected from the wind.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The meteorological conditions will facilitate a gradual strengthening of the near-surface layers in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of the strong wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a storm force northerly wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed in all aspects. In addition further wind slabs will form. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls dry slab avalanches are possible, even quite large ones. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes.
In addition loose snow avalanches are possible.
Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 25 cm of snow has fallen. The wind was violent at times. The wind will transport the new snow significantly.
The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Considerable avalanche danger will persist. The avalanches can in some places be released easily. Restraint is advisable on this first sunny day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: For those venturing off piste a precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a storm force northerly wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed in all aspects. In addition further wind slabs will form. These can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, above the tree line. Avalanches can be triggered in near-surface layers. Additionally in some places avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m.
The danger exists primarily in alpine snow sports terrain. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.

In addition dry loose snow avalanches are to be expected, in the event of solar radiation especially on extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen.
The wind was violent in some cases. On Friday the wind will be strong to storm force. The wind will transport the new snow significantly.

The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Avalanches can as before be released easily. Restraint is advisable on this first sunny day.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow. Wind slabs have formed especially above the tree line.
The avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example, and on wind-loaded slopes.

In particular on steep shady slopes avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

Off-piste activities call for caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow.
As a consequence of sharply falling temperatures and the moderate to strong foehn wind, snow drift accumulations formed.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Wind slabs are lying on the smooth surface of an old snowpack.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
Wind slabs require caution.
Above the tree line the avalanche danger is a little higher.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow. In particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and on wind-loaded slopes medium-sized slab avalanches are possible in particular above the tree line. Some occasionally large avalanches are, however, not ruled out.

Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally, caution is to be exercised in particular on steep slopes, and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Backcountry touring calls for caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The foehn wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly.
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

As a consequence of low temperatures and the moderate to strong northwesterly wind, the snowpack can not consolidate. The weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack in particular at intermediate and high altitudes.

In particular adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The more recent wind slabs represent the main danger. Dry slab avalanches are to be expected. They can be released by small loads. Medium-sized avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes. This applies in all aspects above approximately 1800 m, caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack on very steep shady slopes. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects, caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. They can also release deeper layers of the snowpack. Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. In particular slopes that have been little used this winter thus far where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in all aspects above approximately 1800 m.

Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above the tree line.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh wind slabs can be released easily by a single winter sport participant in all aspects above approximately 1800 m. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Avalanches are medium-sized.

In isolated cases avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow has fallen. As a consequence of the gusty wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally.

The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above approximately 1800 m. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes individual small to medium-sized slab avalanches are possible above approximately 1300 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line. These can be released in near-surface layers. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes.
Individual natural avalanches are not ruled out.

snowpackStructure.comment: 5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen. The wind will be storm force in some cases. The northwesterly wind will transport the new snow. Avalanche prone wind slabs will form.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow. Wind slabs have formed especially above the tree line.
Wind slabs can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example, and on steep slopes.
In particular in gullies and bowls avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.

Off-piste activities call for caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow.
As a consequence of sharply falling temperatures and the moderate to strong foehn wind, snow drift accumulations formed.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Wind slabs are lying on the smooth surface of an old snowpack.

In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1300 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs in the early morning. The new snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes storm force wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in the course of the day in all aspects. In particular in northeast to east to southwest facing aspects individual mostly small slab avalanches are possible as the snowfall becomes more intense.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow will be deposited on the quite favourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
